title: mentha arvensis f. nummularia
intro: Mentha arvensis f. nummularia, also known as Japanese peppermint or American wild mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha arvensis plant, a perennial herb native to North America and Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce nausea and vomiting, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis f. nummularia include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, as well as its ability to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Mentha arvensis f. nummularia can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and it is also used in traditional medicine and as a flavoring agent in foods and beverages.
